name: Grand Marnier Chocolate Truffles
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces good quality bittersweet chocolate (I use Callebaut)
1/4 cup liqueur (or an additional 1/4 cup cream if you don't want to use liqueur - I often use 6 tablespoons liqueur. It depends on the liqueur.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop 12 ounces chocolate into chunks.
2. Place chocolate pieces in a food processor with a metal blade and process until the chocolate is finely chopped.
3. Bring cream to a boil.
4. With the food processor running, add cream to chocolate and process until the cream is completely incorporated.
5. With the processor still running, add liqueur and process until incorporated.
6. Refrigerate the chocolate mixture until thoroughly chilled.
7. Using powdered sugar on your hands to prevent the chocolate from sticking, roll teaspoonsful of the chocolate mixture into balls.
8. Refrigerate again until chilled.
9. Melt 1 pound chocolate in the top of a double boiler. Allow chocolate to cool to about 90 degrees F.
10. Take each chocolate ball and dip it in the melted chocolate.
11. Place the chocolate ball in the cocoa to coat.
12. Refrigerate once again.
13. Store truffles in the refrigerator. Remove from the refrigerator and allow to rise to room temperature for at least 1 hour before serving.
